close_reason: Not a real issue. None of the AWS backend structs derive Clone; they are always held as Arc<dyn SecretStore>. The SSM backend's bare client field is correct. The Arc wrapping in aws-sm and aws-kms is unnecessary (but harmless). No inconsistency that affects callers.
description: secretx-aws-ssm stores client as aws_sdk_ssm::Client (not Arc-wrapped). secretx-aws-sm stores Arc<aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client> and secretx-aws-kms stores Arc<aws_sdk_kms::Client>. The SDK Client type is Clone but cloning it replicates the internal connection pool and credential state rather than sharing it. A next-sibling crate author following the SSM pattern will omit Arc and silently replicate connection pools on clone. The Arc pattern in SM and KMS is the intended one: share one client across clones of the backend struct. Fix: wrap AwsSsmBackend.client in Arc<aws_sdk_ssm::Client> to match the pattern established by the other two AWS backends.
title: AwsSsmBackend.client not Arc-wrapped unlike AwsSm and AwsKms
